stage: S5 (Multi-board Depth & Benefit Parity) / P02 精确、结构化、全文与历史检索

value.user_value: An FTS benchmark + semantic-rerank experiment + adopt/reject ADR. On a fixed query set, FTS (deterministic, 0 external infra) is measured (MRR/recall), and three candidate semantic rerankers are benchmarked: a synonym-aware one that genuinely improves recall (0.875 -> 0.917) is ADOPTED; a non-improving one and a structured-filter-bypassing one are both REJECTED. Semantic/vector infrastructure is introduced only when it earns its cost -- never prematurely.
value.why_it_matters: Competitors reach for semantic/vector search first and pay in cost, latency, and lost exactness. The discipline here -- adopt semantic ONLY if it beats FTS on a fixed set AND still respects structured filters -- is what keeps retrieval both deep and deterministic.

cost.note: unknown != 0; FTS is in-process term-overlap with 0 external infra; a semantic layer would add index/query/latency/infra cost, justified only by the ADR rule.

deployment: NOT_DEPLOYED (FTS benchmark + semantic experiment + ADR; production worker/cron/data untouched, live build unchanged b189d3cc0703 == T040)
